value_accrual_summary: AAVE stakers in the Safety Module earn yield that is primarily funded by inflationary AAVE token emissions (not protocol revenue), while a portion of protocol interest-rate fees flow to the DAO treasury via the Ecosystem Reserve and Collector contract, with no direct fee distribution to stakers or token burns as of mid-2025; Aave v3 'Umbrella' upgrade (in governance) proposes shifting Safety Module rewards toward real fee-backed yield.

_notes.staking_apr_source: Live APR pulled from app.aave.com/staking as of research date; fluctuates daily. The ~4-5% figure is denominated in AAVE tokens emitted from the Ecosystem Reserve, NOT from protocol fee revenue.
_notes.fee_flow: Protocol fees (reserve factor from borrowers) accumulate in the Aave Collector contract controlled by governance. They are NOT automatically distributed to AAVE stakers; governance votes on their use (grants, buybacks, treasury). No burn mechanism exists in deployed contracts.
_notes.umbrella_caveat: Aave Governance Proposal 'Umbrella' (ARFC posted 2024-Q4) proposes replacing the current Safety Module with a fee-backed slashing/reward system. Not yet live as of mid-2025.
_notes.inflationary_flag_rationale: StakedAave (stkAAVE) rewards are sourced from the AaveEcosystemReserve (pre-minted AAVE supply allocation), making them dilutive emissions, not cash-flow yield.
